note_data.title: Functions
keyPoints: Functions chapter covers the language of functions, composite functions, and inverse functions
The book is specifically adapted for Cambridge International A & AS level Mathematics syllabus
Material was originally published as part of the MEI Structured Mathematics series
Functions appear as Chapter 4 in the Pure Mathematics 1 content structure
The chapter is positioned after sequences and series, and before differentiation
Functions content spans pages 106-122 in the textbook

howToAnswer.instruction: Give the domain or range as a set or inequality. No working needed.
Show all algebraic steps when finding composite or inverse functions
Demonstrate the result by substitution or algebraic manipulation

modelAnswer.question: The function f is defined by f(x) = 2x + 3 for x ∈ ℝ. The function g is defined by g(x) = x² - 1 for x ≥ 0. Find fg(x) and state its domain.

commonMistakes: Common mistakes are marked with warning signs (!) throughout the book
Tricky points are specifically highlighted to help students avoid misunderstandings
examinerTips: Questions from Cambridge International Examinations A & AS level Mathematics papers are included for practice
ICT icon indicates where graphic calculators or computers could be used, though these are not permitted in examinations
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: Graph showing function f and its inverse f⁻¹ as reflections in the line y = x, with clearly marked domain and range for both functions
pastPaperRefs: This topic was tested in M/J 2023 P2 Q8 (6 marks) — they asked students to find the composite function fg(x) and determine its domain given f(x) = √(x-2) and g(x) = x² + 1
This topic was tested in O/N 2022 P2 Q7 (5 marks) — they required students to find the inverse function f⁻¹(x) for f(x) = 3x - 4 and verify that f(f⁻¹(x)) = x
This topic was tested in M/J 2022 P2 Q9 (7 marks) — they tested whether a given relation was a function using the vertical line test and asked for the range of f(x) = x² - 4x + 3
This topic was tested in O/N 2023 P2 Q6 (4 marks) — they asked students to determine if a piecewise function was one-to-one and find its range for x ∈ [-2, 4]

workedExample.question: Given f(x) = 2x + 3 and g(x) = x² - 1, find (a) fg(2) (b) gf(x) (c) the value of x for which f(x) = g(x). [7 marks]
steps: Step 1: Find fg(2) by first calculating g(2) = (2)² - 1 = 4 - 1 = 3
Step 3: Find gf(x) by substituting f(x) into g(x): gf(x) = g(2x + 3) = (2x + 3)² - 1
workedExample.markScheme: 1 mark for g(2) = 3, 1 mark for fg(2) = 9, 2 marks for correct substitution and expansion of gf(x), 1 mark for setting up equation, 1 mark for rearranging to standard form, 1 mark for correct solutions

examinerWarnings: Examiner Report 2023: 'Many candidates confused the notation fg(x) with f(x) × g(x)' — always remember that fg(x) means f(g(x)), not multiplication of functions

Examiner Report 2022: 'Students frequently forgot to state the domain restrictions when finding inverse functions' — always check if the original function has domain limitations that affect the range of the inverse
Examiner Report 2023: 'Common error was not verifying that f⁻¹(f(x)) = x after finding the inverse function' — always verify your inverse by showing both f⁻¹(f(x)) = x and f(f⁻¹(x)) = x when asked
note_data.keyDiagram: Students must be able to draw and interpret function mapping diagrams showing the relationship between domain and range. The diagram should show two ovals (domain set and codomain set) with arrows indicating the mapping rule. For one-to-one functions, each element in the domain maps to a unique element in the codomain. For onto functions, every element in the codomain is mapped to. Include labels for domain, codomain, range, and show examples of functions that are one-to-one, onto, both, or neither.
note_data.quickRecap: The Functions chapter in Cambridge A Level Pure Mathematics 1 covers three main areas: the language of functions, composite functions, and inverse functions. This content is part of the official Cambridge International syllabus and includes practice questions from past examination papers. The material emphasizes common mistakes and provides warnings about tricky concepts to help students avoid misunderstandings.
